normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU2_feasibility_witness {n : Nat} (intervals : Fin n → (Int × Int)) (ans : Nat) : (∀ i : Fin n, let iv := intervals i; iv.1.1 < iv.1.2) → ans = greedy_interval_scheduling_001 intervals → ∃ (S : Finset (Fin n)), (S.card = ans) ∧ (∀ i j ∈ S, i ≠ j → nonOverlapping (intervals i) (intervals j))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned count is realized by some subset of the input intervals that are pairwise non-overlapping.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU3_optimality_maximal {n : Nat} (intervals : Fin n → (Int × Int)) (ans : Nat) : (∀ i : Fin n, let iv := intervals i; iv.1.1 < iv.1.2) → ans = greedy_interval_scheduling_001 intervals → (∀ (S : Finset (Fin n))), (∀ i j ∈ S, i ≠ j → nonOverlapping (intervals i) (intervals j)) → S.card ≤ ans
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Optimality: no pairwise non-overlapping subset is strictly larger than the returned count.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU5_empty_input_zero (intervals : Fin 0 → (Int × Int)) : greedy_interval_scheduling_001 intervals = 0
theorem SU4_endpoint_convention_nonOverlapping_shared_endpoint {a b c : Int} : b ≤ c → nonOverlapping (a, b) (b, c)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Endpoint convention: intervals that only share an endpoint are considered non-overlapping for the [start,end) model.
